comments.author: Alice Chen
comments.body: Throwing here means any request with a missing env var crashes the process. Should this return null instead and log a warning?

comments.author: Bob Park
comments.body: Intentional — a missing secret is a deployment error, not a runtime condition. Fail-fast is safer than silently accepting all tokens.

comments.author: Alice Chen
comments.body: Fair point. Can we at least validate at startup instead of on first request? That way the process fails immediately on boot.

comments.author: Carol Martinez
comments.body: Bumping salt rounds from 10 to 12 roughly quadruples hashing time. Have you benchmarked this on the auth service? We're already close to the 200ms p99 budget.

comments.author: Bob Park
comments.body: Benchmarked on staging — p99 went from 85ms to 140ms. Still within budget, and OWASP recommends 12 as of 2025.

comments.author: Alice Chen
comments.body: request.ip can be spoofed via X-Forwarded-For if we're behind a reverse proxy. Are we trusting the proxy?

comments.author: Bob Park
comments.body: Good catch. We need to set `trust proxy` on the Express app. I'll add that in a follow-up since it affects the server config.

comments.author: Carol Martinez
comments.body: The Object.fromEntries cast is a bit opaque. Would a plain object literal with the four methods be clearer? It's only four lines.
